title: LibreTranslate API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary and key terms for the LibreTranslate machine translation API

terms.definition: The language code of the text being translated, or 'auto' to enable automatic language detection
The language code of the desired output language for translation
A BCP 47 short code identifying a language (e.g. 'en' for English, 'es' for Spanish, 'fr' for French)
The capability to automatically identify the source language of input text without the caller specifying it; triggered by passing 'auto' as the source parameter
A numeric score (0–100) indicating how certain the model is about a detected language
Additional candidate translations returned alongside the primary translation; requested via the 'alternatives' integer parameter
The markup format of input text; either 'text' for plain text or 'html' to preserve HTML tags during translation
A UUID token issued by the LibreTranslate portal that authenticates API requests and grants access to higher rate-limit tiers
The open-source offline translation library that powers LibreTranslate, based on the OpenNMT neural machine translation framework
A deployment mode in which the LibreTranslate server is run on private infrastructure, requiring no external network calls for translation
The endpoint that accepts a document file (PDF, DOCX, etc.) as a multipart upload and returns a URL to the translated output file
A user-submitted correction or improvement to an existing translation pair, submitted via the /suggest endpoint to improve future model quality
The maximum number of characters accepted per translation request on a given instance; -1 indicates no limit
A cap on the number of API requests allowed within a time window; LibreTranslate responds with HTTP 429 when exceeded
The GNU Affero General Public License v3 under which LibreTranslate is released, requiring downstream modifications to also be open-sourced
